006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022
023/**
024 * Supported wire formats for {@link Currency} values.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Used by {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#currencyFormat(CurrencyFormat)},
028 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#currencyFormat()},
029 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#currencyFormat()}, and
030 * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#currencyFormat()} to control how {@link Currency}
031 * values are written to text-based wire formats.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * The default is {@link #ISO_CODE} which preserves the historical wire output ({@link Currency#toString()} /
035 * {@link Currency#getCurrencyCode()}).
036 *
037 * <h5 class='topic'>Locale composability</h5>
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * {@link #SYMBOL} and {@link #DISPLAY_NAME} are locale-sensitive — they consult the supplied {@link Locale}
041 * (typically the {@link MarshallingContext#getLocale()} resolved through the {@link MarshallingSession}).
042 * {@link #ISO_CODE} ignores the locale.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='topic'>Round-trip caveat</h5>
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * <b>Round-trip is strictly guaranteed only for {@link #ISO_CODE}.</b>  {@code SYMBOL} parsing of a token
048 * like {@code "$"} could resolve to any of USD / CAD / AUD / MXN / HKD / SGD / NZD depending on the
049 * locale, and {@code DISPLAY_NAME} parsing is even less reliable ({@code "Dollar"} matches at least eight
050 * ISO codes). For machine-to-machine wires, prefer {@link #ISO_CODE}.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='topic'>Precedence (highest to lowest)</h5>
053 * <ol>
054 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledProp#currencyFormat() @MarshalledProp(currencyFormat=…)} on the bean property.
055 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Marshalled#currencyFormat() @Marshalled(currencyFormat=…)} on the bean class.
056 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshalledConfig#currencyFormat() @MarshalledConfig(currencyFormat=…)} on
057 *       <code><ja>@Rest</ja></code>-annotated classes / methods.
058 *    <li>Programmatic {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#currencyFormat(CurrencyFormat)}.
059 *    <li>Environment variable <c>MarshallingContext.currencyFormat</c>.
060 *    <li>The default constant ({@link #ISO_CODE}).
061 * </ol>
062 *
063 * <h5 class='topic'>Parser leniency</h5>
064 *
065 * <p>
066 * Parsers SHALL try {@link Currency#getInstance(String)} first regardless of the parser-side
067 * {@code CurrencyFormat} setting — this handles the {@link #ISO_CODE} shape unambiguously. For
068 * {@link #SYMBOL} / {@link #DISPLAY_NAME}, the parser falls back to a best-effort iterate-and-match scan
069 * against {@link Currency#getAvailableCurrencies()}.
070 *
071 * <h5 class='topic'>Binary serializers</h5>
072 *
073 * <p>
074 * Binary serializers (BSON / CBOR / MsgPack / Prototext / Parquet) emit a UTF-8 string regardless of this
075 * setting — there is no native {@code Currency} wire type in any supported binary format. The choice of
076 * {@code ISO_CODE} vs {@code SYMBOL} vs {@code DISPLAY_NAME} is purely about the textual content of that
077 * string.
078 */
079public enum CurrencyFormat {
080
081   /** Sentinel meaning "no value configured" — falls through to the next-higher precedence level. */
082   NOT_SET,
083
084   /**
085    * ISO 4217 currency code (the default).
086    *
087    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
088    * <p class='bcode'>
089    *    USD
090    *    EUR
091    *    JPY
092    * </p>
093    *
094    * <p>
095    * Locale-independent. Round-trips through {@link Currency#getInstance(String)}.
096    */
097   ISO_CODE,
098
099   /**
100    * Currency symbol per {@link Currency#getSymbol(Locale)}.
101    *
102    * <h5 class='figure'>Example (locale {@code en_US}):</h5>
103    * <p class='bcode'>
104    *    $
105    *    €
106    *    ¥
107    * </p>
108    *
109    * <p>
110    * <b>Locale-sensitive.</b> The symbol is resolved against the supplied locale.  Round-trip is
111    * best-effort — {@code "$"} is shared by USD / CAD / AUD / MXN / HKD / SGD / NZD; the parser prefers
112    * the locale's default currency on ambiguity and throws when no match is found.
113    */
114   SYMBOL,
115
116   /**
117    * Localized display name per {@link Currency#getDisplayName(Locale)}.
118    *
119    * <h5 class='figure'>Example (locale {@code en_US}):</h5>
120    * <p class='bcode'>
121    *    US Dollar
122    *    Euro
123    *    Japanese Yen
124    * </p>
125    *
126    * <p>
127    * <b>Locale-sensitive and even less round-trip-safe than {@link #SYMBOL}.</b>  Use only for
128    * user-facing display contexts.
129    */
130   DISPLAY_NAME;
131
132   /**
133    * Formats the specified {@link Currency} using this format.
134    *
135    * <p>
136    * {@link #NOT_SET} falls through to {@link #ISO_CODE}. {@code null} locale is treated as
137    * {@link Locale#ROOT}.
138    *
139    * @param value The value to format. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
140    * @param format The configured format. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #ISO_CODE}).
141    * @param locale The locale used by {@link #SYMBOL} / {@link #DISPLAY_NAME}. Can be <jk>null</jk>
142    *   (treated as {@link Locale#ROOT}).
143    * @return The formatted wire representation, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk>.
144    */
145   public static String format(Currency value, CurrencyFormat format, Locale locale) {
146      if (value == null)
147         return null;
148      var fmt = format == null ? ISO_CODE : format;
149      var loc = locale == null ? Locale.ROOT : locale;
150      return switch (fmt) {
151         case NOT_SET, ISO_CODE -> value.getCurrencyCode();
152         case SYMBOL -> value.getSymbol(loc);
153         case DISPLAY_NAME -> value.getDisplayName(loc);
154      };
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Parses the specified wire value into a {@link Currency}.
159    *
160    * <p>
161    * Lenient parsing — always tries {@link Currency#getInstance(String)} first (handles {@link #ISO_CODE}
162    * unambiguously).  For {@link #SYMBOL} / {@link #DISPLAY_NAME} the parser falls back to a best-effort
163    * iterate-and-match scan against {@link Currency#getAvailableCurrencies()}; when multiple currencies
164    * match a symbol, prefers the locale's default currency.
165    *
166    * @param value The wire value. Can be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
167    * @param format The configured format hint. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link #ISO_CODE}).
168    * @param locale The locale used to disambiguate {@link #SYMBOL} / {@link #DISPLAY_NAME} matches.
169    *   Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as {@link Locale#ROOT}).
170    * @return The parsed {@link Currency}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
171    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the value cannot be resolved to a unique currency.
172    */
173   public static Currency parse(String value, CurrencyFormat format, Locale locale) {
174      if (value == null)
175         return null;
176      var s = value.trim();
177      if (s.isEmpty())
178         return null;
179      var loc = locale == null ? Locale.ROOT : locale;
180      var fmt = format == null ? ISO_CODE : format;
181      // Try ISO code first — works unambiguously for the ISO_CODE shape and many SYMBOL / DISPLAY_NAME inputs.
182      try {
183         return Currency.getInstance(s);
184      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IllegalArgumentException ignored) {
185         // Fall through to symbol / display-name scan.
186      }
187      return switch (fmt) {
188         case NOT_SET, ISO_CODE -> throw illegalArg("Invalid currency code ''{0}''", value);
189         case SYMBOL -> findBySymbol(s, loc, value);
190         case DISPLAY_NAME -> findByDisplayName(s, loc, value);
191      };
192   }
193
194   private static Currency findBySymbol(String token, Locale loc, String original) {
195      var matches = new ArrayList<Currency>();
196      for (var c : Currency.getAvailableCurrencies())
197         if (token.equals(c.getSymbol(loc)))
198            matches.add(c);
199      return resolveAmbiguousMatches(matches, loc, original, "symbol");
200   }
201
202   private static Currency findByDisplayName(String token, Locale loc, String original) {
203      var matches = new ArrayList<Currency>();
204      for (var c : Currency.getAvailableCurrencies())
205         if (token.equals(c.getDisplayName(loc)))
206            matches.add(c);
207      return resolveAmbiguousMatches(matches, loc, original, "display name");
208   }
209
210   private static Currency resolveAmbiguousMatches(List<Currency> matches, Locale loc, String original, String kind) {
211      if (matches.isEmpty())
212         throw illegalArg("Could not resolve currency {0} ''{1}'' in locale {2}", kind, original, loc);
213      if (matches.size() == 1)
214         return matches.get(0);
215      // Prefer the locale's default currency when the locale has one.
216      try {
217         var preferred = Currency.getInstance(loc);
218         if (matches.contains(preferred))
219            return preferred;
220      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IllegalArgumentException ignored) {
221         // Locale has no associated currency (e.g. Locale.ROOT, Locale.ENGLISH, Locale.JAPAN may or may not).
222      }
223      throw illegalArg("Ambiguous currency {0} ''{1}'' in locale {2}: matches {3}", kind, original, loc, matches);
224   }
225
226   /**
227    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this format emits a numeric wire value.
228    *
229    * <p>
230    * Always <jk>false</jk> — every constant emits a textual representation.
231    *
232    * @return <jk>false</jk>.
233    */
234   @SuppressWarnings({
235      "static-method", // Kept as an instance method for polymorphic-by-convention symmetry with the other Format classes (BigNumberFormat, FloatFormat, DurationFormat, etc.) where isNumeric() depends on the enum constant.
236      "java:S3400"     // Same rationale — must remain an instance method, not a constant, to match the cross-Format API contract.
237   })
238   public boolean isNumeric() {
239      return false;
240   }
241}
